title: quercus macrophylla var. rugosa
intro: Quercus macrophylla var. rugosa, also known as the Fagayin or Rough-barked Fagaceae, is a species of evergreen tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and distinctive rough bark. The leaves and bark of Quercus macrophylla var. rugosa have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing blood sugar levels, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting liver function. Quercus macrophylla var. rugosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
